details.found: ...select **Requires authentication** and then select one or more roles...

previousReviewFixVerification.residualScan: grep -in 'choose|chosen|chose' returned ZERO matches. No remaining violations.

invariantResults.evidence: All headings use sentence case: 'Page access levels', 'Configure page security', 'Restrict a page to specific member roles', 'Move pages with access privileges mismatch', 'Next steps'.
Verb-phrase headings use imperative mood: 'Configure page security', 'Restrict a page to specific member roles', 'Move pages with access privileges mismatch'.
No heading starts with 'How to'.
Title 'Secure pages' is unique and unambiguous.
Title and headings contain searchable terms: 'secure pages', 'page security', 'member roles'.
No feature-name capitalization issues found. Xperience capitalized correctly throughout.
Active voice used throughout. Passive on lines 49 ('is selected') and 86 ('is secured') falls under permitted exceptions (b): emphasizing the receiver of the action in conditional explanations.
Present simple tense used consistently. No past tense (was/were/had been) found.
American English spelling used throughout. No British variants found.

'member roles' used consistently in prose. 'Membership roles' (bolded) used only when referencing the actual UI element label, matching the admin UI's ObjectIdSelectorComponent label from KenticoAdminWebsitesResources.resx.
Single 'it' pronoun on line 54: 'all pages located under it' — antecedent 'the current page' is clear from same sentence. Acceptable.
Indicative and imperative mood used. No subjunctive forms found.
No gendered pronouns found. N/A for this content.
No culturally specific idioms, slang, or metaphors found.
No redundant information. The two procedures (general configure + role-specific) serve distinct purposes without duplicating explanations.
Concise phrasing throughout. No unnecessarily long phrases.
No filler words (actually, basically, really, just, in fact) found.
Most sentences under 20 words. Lines 23 and 86 are longer but serve as complete definitions/explanations where splitting would reduce clarity.
Specific language used throughout. No vague terms like 'some', 'certain', or 'various'.
No instances of 'please' found.
Content is direct and concise. Sections get to the point quickly.
Simple English used throughout. Accessible to non-native speakers.
No (s) optional plural constructs found.
Conditions lead instructions: line 42 'To make the page accessible…, select'; line 43 'To restrict the page…, select'; line 67 'To ensure that only members…'.
Page is self-contained. Key concepts (three-tier model) explained on-page. Cross-references provided to Members, Registration, and developer reflection pages.
No heading ends with -ing form.
'Select' used for all UI interactions: buttons (lines 53, 74), checkboxes (lines 42, 72), list items (lines 38, 70, 73). No 'Click' found.
N/A — no checkbox unchecking instructions in this page.
N/A — no typing/entering value instructions in this page.
'Open' used correctly for applications on lines 37, 69, 80.
'Switch to' used for tab navigation on lines 39 and 71. Acceptable synonym for 'Go to' in tab context.
No 'Press' used. N/A — no keyboard interactions.
No 'Click' found anywhere in the document.
No 'Enable/Disable' used for UI elements. N/A.
No 'Check/Uncheck' found.
VERIFIED: All 4 previous violations corrected. grep returns zero matches for choose/chosen/chose. Lines 43, 49, 73, 76 all use 'select/selected'.
Instructions are action-focused. 'Select **Publish change**' (lines 53, 74) names the specific button, which is appropriate for business users who need precise UI guidance.
'you can' on line 86 describes optional choices when resolving access mismatch — permitted per exception for optional steps.
Line 29 'must also' refers to the development team, not the reader directly. Acceptable per rule: 'must is acceptable when not writing about people' (here it addresses the team as a role).
No 'have to' found.

No contractions used. The page maintains a consistent formal register throughout. While contractions are encouraged (should-level), the consistent absence is acceptable and does not breach a must-severity rule.
No awkward contractions found.
No double contractions found.
No noun+verb contractions found.
N/A — no UI text with mixed contractions.

Lines 39, 71, 80 use Unicode arrow '→' for navigation paths. The invariant specifies '->' characters, but '→' is the established convention across all peer documents in the repository (manage-page-urls.md, content-sync.md, etc.). Only one file in the entire business-users collection uses '->'. Flagged as a note rather than a failure because: (1) pre-existing pattern not introduced in this task, (2) codebase-wide convention, (3) changing would create inconsistency with peer pages.
invariantResults.note: See additionalFindings for detailed recommendation on arrow notation.

invariantResults.evidence: Lines 39/71: **Properties** → **Security** — both are navigation panes/tabs within the page editor (same UI type). Line 80: **Members** → **List of membership roles** — application → section, but follows the style guide exception for navigation to settings/listings where the UI type is included in the label.
No invented abbreviations. 'i.e.' on line 22 is standard.
No three-dot misuse found.
No possessive constructs that should be X of Y. N/A.
No 'this guide' or 'In this guide' references.
No jargon, metaphors, or complex expressions. Technical terms delegated to development team (line 29).

All required elements present: Title (front matter 'Secure pages'), Introduction (lines 13-25: info box + Page access levels), Body (lines 33-92: two procedures + mismatch section), Result (lines 57, 76: end-result descriptions), Next Steps (lines 94-97).
Introduction (lines 17-25) provides context about page access, describes the three-tier model with real-world framing (public content, members-only, exclusive content for specific groups).
Introduction answers: What (restrict page access to specific audiences), Why (create a mix of public and restricted content), When (lines 21-23 describe the three scenarios).
Ordered lists used for both step-by-step procedures (lines 37-55, 69-74). Bullet points used for unordered options (lines 21-23, 42-43, 88-90, 96-97).

One action per step in both procedures. Steps 1-5 in 'Configure page security' and steps 1-6 in 'Restrict a page' each contain a single action. Step 4 sub-bullets describe options within one configuration action, not separate sequential steps.
Both procedures are under 8 steps: Configure (5 steps), Restrict (6 steps).
End results described: Line 57 ('The page's security settings are now updated. A lock icon appears…') and line 76 ('The page is now restricted to the selected member roles…').
Next steps section present (lines 94-97) linking to Member roles and Secure content items.
Page is self-contained. The three-tier model is explained on-page. External concepts (registration, member roles, developer implementation) are cross-referenced with page_link tags.
Happy path assumed throughout. Prerequisites noted in info box (line 29) without over-explaining setup.
No redundant intro sentences after headings. Lead-in sentences (lines 35, 67) provide necessary context before procedures.
Headings use general terms: 'Page access levels', 'Configure page security', 'Next steps'. No Kentico-specific jargon in headings.
All list items start with capitalized first letter.
Good use of lists (procedures, options), callout boxes (5 info boxes), and images (3 image tags) to break up content.
Task-oriented approach: 'Configure page security' and 'Restrict a page to specific member roles' describe user scenarios, not feature descriptions.
No over-explanation. Concepts introduced concisely. Info boxes used for supplementary details.
Maximum list depth is 2 (step → sub-bullet on lines 42-43, 54-55). No 3+ level nesting.
All page_link tags use identifiers: permissions_pagelevel_xp, BIsuCw, tYouCw, 4obWCQ, content_items_xp. Identifier correctness is accuracy-reviewer's domain.
Source file is in _documentation collection. page_link tags do not specify collection, defaulting to the source collection. Standard practice.
Anchors used: 'Member roles', 'Retrieve page data', 'Secure content items'. Anchor existence verification is accuracy-reviewer's domain.
Anchor values in page_link tags use letters, spaces (which is normal for the anchor attribute in this tag system), and hyphens. Format is correct.
No external links present. N/A.

All page_link tags use descriptive linkText: 'members', 'member roles', 'Registration and authentication', 'reflect the settings', 'Member roles', 'Secure content items'. Line 14's page_link without linkText will render the target page's title, which is descriptive by definition.
Admonition tags use valid Liquid syntax: 5 instances of {% info %}...{% endinfo %} on lines 13, 27, 47, 59, 78. All properly closed.

Image tags use proper format with border=true for UI screenshots: SecurityTab.png (line 40, width=500), secure_subpages_modal.png (line 55, width=250), image-2023-10-6_10-5-34.png (line 92, width=500). All have title attributes.
Front matter related_pages contains identifiers from the documentation collection: barWCQ, content_items_xp, BIsuCw, member_roles_xp.

additionalFindings.evidence: Heading progression: h2 (line 17) → h2 (line 33) → h2 (line 65) → h3 (line 84) → h2 (line 94). No skipped levels. h3 correctly nested under h2.

Lines 39, 71, 80 use Unicode '→' instead of ASCII '->'. INV-style-046 specifies '->'. However, '→' is the de facto codebase standard — peer documents manage-page-urls.md (3 instances), content-sync.md (1 instance) all use '→'. Only content-item-assets.md uses '->'. Changing this page alone would create inconsistency within the business-users collection.
additionalFindings.suggestion: If the team decides to enforce '->' per the style guide, it should be a codebase-wide bulk fix, not a per-task change. No action needed for this task.

additionalFindings.evidence: No contractions used throughout the page. INV-style-041 (should-level) recommends common contractions for a friendly, informal tone. The consistent absence is not a violation but the page could benefit from contractions like 'don't', 'isn't', 'can't' to align with the business-user friendly tone (INV-persona-001).
additionalFindings.suggestion: Consider adding contractions in future iterations for warmer tone, e.g., line 23 'do not have' → 'don't have', line 76 'cannot view' → 'can't view'.

additionalFindings.evidence: All Liquid tags properly opened and closed. 5 info blocks, 3 image tags, 8 page_link tags, 1 icon tag. No broken includes or mismatched delimiters.
Required fields present: title, persona, identifier, order, license, redirect_from, related_pages. Values use expected formats.

Bold formatting consistently applied to UI element names (**Requires authentication**, **Membership roles**, **Publish change**, **Properties**, **Security**, **Members**). Info boxes consistently formatted with blank lines after opening tag.
No code blocks present. Appropriate for a business-user how-to page.

metaKnowledgeReview.knowledgeBriefStatus: cold-start — no patterns, anti-patterns, style evolutions, or domain insights available
